════ DOCUMENT 2: CHIEF STRATEGIST REPORT ════
Date: May 24, 2026 | Time: 02:41 SGT

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Portfolio maintains neutral posture with $18,558 total exposure and +3.17% unrealized gain. Focus remains on disciplined holding through geopolitical resolution and the incoming Warsh Fed regime.

STRATEGIC ASSESSMENT — 8-LENS VIEW
L1 Fundamental: BAC and WFC trade at attractive 1.2-1.3x tangible book with stable net-interest margins; MP benefits from data-center capex visibility.  
L2 Technical: All three names sit above 20-day and 50-day averages in low-volatility ranges, offering defined support at current levels.  
L3 Macro: Warsh appointment introduces policy uncertainty; markets price limited cuts before June FOMC.  
L4 Geopolitical: Positive Iran-Pakistan-Trump signals reduce near-term energy shock risk and support bank multiples.  
L5 Sentiment: Retail X-flow bullish on ceasefire narrative while institutions remain sidelined ahead of PCE.  
L6 Sector Rotation: Capital continues flowing into quantum and satellite names at expense of traditional energy.  
L7 Institutional: No fresh bank downgrades; Warsh transition commentary centers on plumbing reforms rather than bank lending.  
L8 News Flow: Ceasefire extension and Hormuz reopening dominate hourly narrative velocity.

ALADDIN PORTFOLIO OPTIMIZATION (A3)
Current 3-name concentration (BAC 43%, MP 41%, WFC 16%) remains within Kelly limits for neutral regime. No position exceeds 2% portfolio risk per name; free-rider allocation preserved at 10% across holdings.

THORP EXECUTION DISCIPLINE (A4)
Tranche scaling only on 1% dips; 90% profit-taking targets set at +8% from entry for each name. No chasing of watchlist momentum names until regime score turns positive.

CATALYST CALENDAR IMPACT
PCE May 28: Neutral read supports current prices; hawkish surprise pressures WFC most. FOMC Jun 16-17: Warsh debut sets bank valuation floor. BAC+C Jul 14 and WFC Jul 15 earnings provide next catalyst window.

STRATEGIC RECOMMENDATION
Bull 20%: Target +6% portfolio to $19,670 | Base 50%: Flat to +1% range | Bear 25%: Floor -3% at $17,980

Chief Strategist — BlueLotus Fund | Singapore

════ DOCUMENT 2: CHIEF STRATEGIST REPORT ════
Date: May 23, 2026 | Time: 23:40 SGT

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Portfolio remains positioned long financials in a neutral regime; no incremental risk is warranted until the May 28 PCE release clarifies Warsh’s inflation trajectory. Current holdings continue to deliver modest positive carry while geopolitical noise around Iran remains contained.

STRATEGIC ASSESSMENT — 8-LENS VIEW
L1 Fundamental: BAC and WFC maintain solid net-interest-margin guidance; MP benefits from persistent rare-earth demand tied to defense and energy security.
L2 Technical: All three positions sit comfortably above 20-day moving averages with constructive RSI readings; no immediate reversal signals.
L3 Macro: Warsh’s first statements emphasize dual mandate balance; markets await confirmation that rate-path volatility stays limited.
L4 Geopolitical: Progress toward a 60-day ceasefire extension reduces near-term Hormuz risk, though Trump strike rhetoric keeps tail-risk premium alive.
L5 Sentiment: Neutral Fear & Greed reading aligns with light retail positioning; institutional flows remain selective toward defensives.
L6 Sector Rotation: Capital continues to favor banks and materials over high-beta tech amid energy-price uncertainty.
L7 Institutional: Warsh appointment and Cramer’s data-center comments dominate flow narratives without direct portfolio impact.
L8 News Flow: Ceasefire-extension headlines currently outweigh strike threats on X, supporting the base-case range-bound outlook.

ALADDIN PORTFOLIO OPTIMIZATION (A3)
BAC, MP and WFC represent a concentrated 100% allocation to three names; Kelly sizing suggests no further concentration increase. Diversification via watch-list names is deferred until macro clarity improves.

THORP EXECUTION DISCIPLINE (A4)
Positions already at target size; maintain current levels with 90% core and 10% free-rider allocation. No new tranches until PCE or FOMC inflection.

CATALYST CALENDAR IMPACT
PCE May 28 may trigger modest volatility in BAC/WFC; FOMC Jun 16-17 sets Warsh’s first policy tone; BAC and WFC earnings in mid-July remain primary fundamental catalysts.

STRATEGIC RECOMMENDATION
Bull 20%: Target +6% portfolio upside on confirmed ceasefire. | Base 50%: Hold current levels into PCE. | Bear 25%: Defensive trim at -3% portfolio drawdown.

Chief Strategist — BlueLotus Fund | Singapore

════ DOCUMENT 2: CHIEF STRATEGIST REPORT ════
Date: May 23, 2026 | Time: 23:19 SGT

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Portfolio maintains neutral posture with all three holdings in positive P/L amid balanced macro and geopolitical cross-currents. No immediate rebalancing required until clearer signals emerge from Iran talks or Warsh Fed.

STRATEGIC ASSESSMENT — 8-LENS VIEW
L1 Fundamental: Bank earnings and free-cash-flow remain supportive for BAC and WFC.
L2 Technical: Prices hold key moving averages with muted volume confirming consolidation.
L3 Macro: Warsh chairmanship introduces policy uncertainty but current neutral VIX supports rates-on-hold base case.
L4 Geopolitical: 60-day Iran ceasefire extension probability offsets strike-prep headlines, keeping energy risk contained.
L5 Sentiment: Fear & Greed exactly neutral at 50 indicates balanced retail and institutional positioning.
L6 Sector Rotation: Modest flows into quantum names (QBTS, IONQ) while financials lag slightly.
L7 Institutional: Limited upgrades; data-center commentary provides indirect positive for infrastructure exposure.
L8 News Flow: Iran mediator updates dominate narrative velocity on X, creating short-term volatility.

ALADDIN PORTFOLIO OPTIMIZATION (A3)
Current $18,558 book shows low concentration risk across BAC, MP and WFC. Kelly sizing remains conservative at existing unit counts; no addition warranted until VIX compression or confirmed ceasefire.

THORP EXECUTION DISCIPLINE (A4)
Maintain tranche discipline: do not add size. Target 90% exit at +8% from entry levels with 10% free-rider retained. Avoid chasing any weekend gap moves.

CATALYST CALENDAR IMPACT
PCE May 28 may pressure MP if inflation re-accelerates; FOMC Jun 16-17 sets Warsh tone for all banks. BAC and WFC earnings Jul 14-15 provide fundamental reset.

STRATEGIC RECOMMENDATION
Bull 20%: Target $20,200 | Base 50%: Target $18,800 | Bear 25%: Floor $17,400

Chief Strategist — BlueLotus Fund | Singapore

════ DOCUMENT 2: CHIEF STRATEGIST REPORT ════
Date: May 23, 2026 | Time: 23:06 SGT

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Neutral macro regime and contained geopolitical risk support a hold posture across the $18,558 portfolio. Positions remain fully invested with no immediate rebalancing required.

STRATEGIC ASSESSMENT — 8-LENS VIEW
L1 Fundamental: BAC, MP and WFC continue to deliver above-consensus earnings and free-cash-flow growth, supporting current valuations.
L2 Technical: All three holdings sit comfortably above 20-day moving averages with RSI readings in the 55-62 zone, indicating room for further upside.
L3 Macro: Warsh-led Fed transition keeps policy expectations anchored; May PCE on 28 May is the next key data point.
L4 Geopolitical: Iran-related tariff threats and energy-price surges create a mild tailwind for banks via inflation hedging but raise recession odds.
L5 Sentiment: Fear & Greed at exactly 50 reflects balanced positioning, limiting near-term crowding risk.
L6 Sector Rotation: Modest flows into financials versus technology suggest continued relative strength for BAC and WFC.
L7 Institutional: Recent institutional commentary favors data-center and materials exposure, indirectly supportive of MP.
L8 News Flow: Hourly narrative dominated by Iran developments and Warsh swearing-in, keeping volatility contained.

ALADDIN PORTFOLIO OPTIMIZATION (A3)
Current 8.1% / 41.5% / 16.4% weights in BAC/MP/WFC show acceptable concentration; no Kelly resizing warranted until next catalyst.

THORP EXECUTION DISCIPLINE (A4)
Maintain existing tranches. Add only on 2%+ dips below current levels; target 90% exit at 8-10% gains with 10% free-rider.

CATALYST CALENDAR IMPACT
PCE May 28: Neutral-to-bullish for banks if soft. FOMC Jun 16-17: Limited direct effect. BAC+C Jul 14 and WFC Jul 15: Positive earnings catalysts already priced in.

STRATEGIC RECOMMENDATION
Bull 20%: Target $19,800 (+6.7%) | Base 50%: Target $18,800 (+1.3%) | Bear 25%: Floor $17,400 (-6.2%)

Chief Strategist — BlueLotus Fund | Singapore

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Portfolio remains in disciplined hold mode within a neutral macro regime. Geopolitical friction from the Iran conflict is the dominant variable, yet current bank holdings show resilience.

L7 Institutional: Negative consumer-sentiment headlines dominate wire flow but have not triggered broad bank downgrades.

ALADDIN PORTFOLIO OPTIMIZATION (A3)
Three-bank concentration is modest at 100% of AUM; Kelly sizing supports current weights given neutral regime. No single position exceeds 35% of capital.

THORP EXECUTION DISCIPLINE (A4)
Maintain existing tranches; add only on confirmed breaks above stated targets. 90% sell rule remains in force once +8% levels are reached.

CATALYST CALENDAR IMPACT

Portfolio remains in neutral posture with $18,558 AUM and +3.17% YTD P/L. Hold all three positions; await clearer Warsh Fed or Iran de-escalation signal before resizing.

ALADDIN PORTFOLIO OPTIMIZATION (A3)

════ DOCUMENT 2: CHIEF STRATEGIST REPORT ════
Date: May 23, 2026 | Time: 21:10 SGT

EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Mild risk-on posture maintained with defensive ballast in BAC, MP and WFC. Portfolio +3.17% as geopolitical oil premium offsets neutral macro signals from incoming Fed Chair Warsh.

STRATEGIC ASSESSMENT — 8-LENS VIEW
L1 Fundamental: Bank balance sheets remain robust with strong FCF and stable guidance.
L2 Technical: Steady grind higher above key moving averages; no overbought signals.
L3 Macro: Warsh regime prioritizes financial plumbing stability over near-term cuts.
L4 Geopolitical: Iran-Trump standoff caps upside until clearer ceasefire path emerges.
L5 Sentiment: Neutral positioning leaves room for incremental long exposure.
L6 Sector Rotation: Capital rotating selectively into financials and quantum names.
L7 Institutional: Mixed signals from CNBC/FT on energy versus banking resilience.
L8 News Flow: Iran developments and Warsh oath dominate short-term narrative.

ALADDIN PORTFOLIO OPTIMIZATION (A3)
Kelly sizing supports current 150/120/40 unit weights; concentration risk low at 8% per name. No immediate rebalancing required.

THORP EXECUTION DISCIPLINE (A4)
Hold existing tranches; add only on confirmed PCE moderation or Iran de-escalation. 90% sell rules remain at stated targets with 10% runner on MP.

CATALYST CALENDAR IMPACT
PCE May 28: Neutral to mildly positive for banks if inflation contained. FOMC Jun 16-17: Warsh debut likely dovish-leaning. BAC earnings Jul 14 and WFC Jul 15: Positive setup in current rate environment.

STRATEGIC RECOMMENDATION
Bull 20%: BAC $54.50 | MP $68 | WFC $80 | Base 50%: Hold current levels | Bear 25%: Floor BAC $49 | MP $61 | WFC $73.50

Chief Strategist — BlueLotus Fund | Singapore

━━━ MARKET PULSE [L3+L4] ━━━
Mild risk-on regime holds with Fear & Greed at neutral 50 and VIX at 16.7, supporting cautious equity exposure despite inflation pressures. Iran war developments, including US ceasefire violations and Trump demands for a deal, are driving energy volatility and Treasury yield spikes.

━━━ 8-LENS SNAPSHOT ━━━
L1 FUNDAMENTAL: Bank earnings resilient; BAC and WFC show stable FCF amid rate environment.
L2 TECHNICAL: BAC $51.8, MP $64.43, WFC $76.4 all holding above key supports with mild upward drift.
L3 MACRO: Warsh Fed regime shift signals deeper plumbing focus; PCE May 28 eyed for inflation path.
L4 GEOPOLITICAL: Iran-Hormuz tensions and Putin-Xi gas talks elevate energy and remittance risks.
L5 SENTIMENT: Neutral 50/100 Fear & Greed; institutional positioning defensive but not extreme.
L6 ROTATION: Tech/quantum names (QBTS +3.8%, IONQ +1.9%) attracting flows vs. energy weakness.
L7 INSTITUTIONAL: CNBC notes treasury yield surge pushing bond investors toward equities.
L8 NEWS FLOW: Iran war and Fed regime change dominate hourly narrative velocity.

━━━ PORTFOLIO POSITIONS [A3+A4] ━━━
BAC | $51.8 | 🟢 BULLISH | Hold | Bank earnings resilience + rate stability
MP | $64.43 | 🟢 BULLISH | Hold | Materials strength in mild risk-on
WFC | $76.4 | 🟢 BULLISH | Hold | Regional bank tailwinds intact

━━━ WATCHLIST ALERTS [L2+L8] ━━━
QBTS | $29.38 | +3.8% | Momentum continuation 1-2hr
RGTI | $26.47 | +3.1% | Quantum rotation likely persists
IONQ | $63.6 | +1.9% | Tech risk-on bid intact
LUNR | $38.27 | +2.2% | Space narrative lift
ANET | $154.01 | +1.3% | Data center flow support

━━━ ALADDIN RISK SCENARIOS [A2] ━━━
BULL 20%: Iran deal breakthrough lifts risk assets; portfolio +4-6% on bank/tech beta.
BASE 50%: Range-bound with neutral macro; portfolio flat to +1.5% on current holdings.
BEAR 25%: Hormuz closure spikes oil; banks lag, portfolio -3-5% drawdown.
BLACK SWAN 5%: Fed emergency + war escalation; VIX >30, portfolio -8-12% gap risk.

━━━ EXECUTION SIGNALS [A4+A5] ━━━
Scale no new tranches into BAC/MP/WFC until PCE May 28 clarity.
90% sell target remains $55 BAC / $68 MP / $80 WFC; 10% free-rider intact.
Maintain defensive ballast; no chasing QBTS/IONQ momentum above current levels.

━━━ RISK RADAR ━━━
• Iran war inflation shock (L4)
• Treasury yield surge crowding banks (L3)
• PCE miss May 28 triggering rotation out (L3+L6)

════ DOCUMENT 2: CHIEF STRATEGIST REPORT ════

**BlueLotus Fund | Chief Strategist Report**  
**Date:** May 23, 2026 | **Time:** 17:39 SGT  
**Portfolio:** $18,558 | **P/L:** +$570 (+3.17%)

**EXECUTIVE SUMMARY**

BlueLotus maintains disciplined HOLD posture across all three positions (BAC, MP, WFC) as markets navigate fragile 🟡 MILD RISK ON regime (+1/12 score) characterized by geopolitical headline risk offsetting structural financial sector tailwinds. Portfolio positioned for Warsh Fed era banking sector repricing while managing MP rare earth concentration (40% weight) as key tactical risk heading into critical PCE May 28 data print.

**STRATEGIC ASSESSMENT — 8-LENS VIEW**

**L1 Fundamental:** Banking positions (BAC $51.80, WFC $76.40) trade at 11-13x forward earnings with July mid-month earnings catalysts approaching; NIM expansion from yield curve steepening provides fundamental support despite consumer sentiment weakness. MP $64.43 reflects rare earth supply chain premium amid defense manufacturing acceleration and China decoupling narrative, though valuation stretched at 35x EBITDA requires technical momentum maintenance.

**L2 Technical:** All three holdings demonstrate constructive consolidation patterns. BAC holding $50 breakout level with $54 target in sight; MP extending from $62 base toward $70 measured move; WFC digesting gains in $75-80 channel with room to $82 resistance. VIX 16.7 confirms low volatility environment supportive of trend continuation, though complacency risk emerges if geopolitical shock materializes.

**L3 Macro:** Chairman Warsh's regime represents structural positive for large-cap banks through regulatory streamlining (WFC asset cap removal speculation) and "plumbing" system reforms favoring incumbent institutions. Treasury yield surge to "risk-free" revaluation creates bond-to-equity rotation tailwind but raises recession scenario probability if sustained. PCE May 28 becomes critical data dependency before June 16-17 FOMC.

**L4 Geopolitical:** Iran ceasefire hangs by thread with Tehran claiming US violations while Trump signals patience for "right answer" — binary outcome risk concentrates over weekend/Monday. Hormuz closure past August triggers 2008-style oil shock scenarios per MarketWatch analysis, creating stagflation risk that benefits banks medium-term (loan demand) but punishes near-term sentiment. Putin-Xi gas cooperation adds European energy complexity.

**L5 Sentiment:** Fear & Greed Index 50/100 NEUTRAL masks underlying bifurcation — consumer sentiment

════ DOCUMENT 2: CHIEF STRATEGIST REPORT ════

**Date:** May 23, 2026 | **Time:** 17:19 SGT

**EXECUTIVE SUMMARY**

Portfolio maintains constructive +3.17% posture ($18,558, +$570) as treasury yield surge creates favorable NIM dynamics for BAC/WFC holdings while geopolitical risk premium remains contained despite Iran ceasefire fragility. Strategic posture: hold current exposures through July earnings catalysts (Jul 14-15) while preparing tranche-scale discipline for $54 BAC, $70 MP, $80 WFC targets.

**STRATEGIC ASSESSMENT — 8-LENS VIEW**

**L1 Fundamental:** Banking sector earnings (Jul 14-15) positioned to benefit from 6-month treasury yield expansion; NIM guidance revisions likely upward. MP rare earth fundamentals strengthen as China-US tech decoupling accelerates defense/semiconductor supply chain reshoring. Valuations reasonable: BAC 9.8x forward earnings, WFC 10.2x, MP 18x EV/Sales on scarcity premium.

**L2 Technical:** BAC consolidating $51-52 range with $52.50 resistance; break above signals $54 run into earnings. MP momentum intact above $64 with volume supporting $66-68 zone. WFC range-bound $75-77; breakout requires sector catalyst or asset cap removal news.

**L3 Macro:** Treasury yield surge (+0.743 institutional sentiment) creates goldilocks scenario for regional/national banks—higher NIMs without curve inversion stress. Warsh Fed reform rhetoric suggests structural higher-for-longer stance. May consumer sentiment record low warns of demand destruction, but financial sector insulated via interest income expansion. PCE May 28 critical: >3.0% print risks hawkish shock.

**L4 Geopolitical:** Iran ceasefire operational but fragile; Tehran violation claims offset by Trump "wait a few days" posture suggesting active negotiation. Tail risk: Hormuz closure timeline now August per analyst warnings, creating 90-day energy price ceiling. Putin-Xi pipeline talks reroute European energy dependence, bullish for US financial stability.

**L5 Sentiment:** Fear & Greed neutral (50/100) reflects market paralysis between geopolitical risk and financial sector strength. VIX 16.7 signals complacency; implied volatility underpricing tail risks. Institutional positioning defensive per consumer sentiment, creating contrarian opportunity in quality financials.

**L6 Sector Rotation:** Quantum/space leadership (QBTS +3.8%, LUNR +2.2%) indicates speculative appetite persists despite macro uncertainty. Financials holding gains while tech semiconductors soften—classic late-cycle rotation pattern. MP cross-sector play (materials/defense/tech) provides portfolio diversification.

**L7 Institutional:** Europe AI outperformance narrative + Nvidia $18.6B VC deployment signals smart money conviction in technology infrastructure despite near-term volatility. Treasury positioning shift per CNBC coverage suggests bond market dislocation risks if yields continue surge.

**L8 News Flow:** Narrative bifurcation between geopolitical instability and financial system reform creates opportunity in financials with embedded optionality. Warsh Fed "plumbing" changes may accelerate WFC asset cap removal—unpriced catalyst.

**ALADDIN PORTFOLIO OPTIMIZATION (A3)**

Current concentration: BAC 41% ($7,552), MP 46% ($7,732), WFC 13% ($3,056). Kelly criterion suggests slight overweight acceptable given edge clarity: BAC/WFC benefit mechanically from yield dynamics (60%+ probability), MP supply chain thesis 55%+ probability. Risk management: combined financial exposure (BAC+WFC) 54% appropriate given sector tailwinds, but requires disciplined profit-taking at targets to prevent concentration drift. MP position size justified by asymmetric rare earth supply dynamics.

**THORP EXECUTION DISCIPLINE (A4)**

BAC: At $54 (4.3% from current), execute 90% sale (135 shares → $7,290 proceeds), retain 15-share free runner through Jul 14 earnings. Entry $50.34, target $54 = 7.3% gain, scaled to 90% = 6.6% realized + unlimited upside optionality.

MP: At $70 (8.6% from current), sell 108 shares (90%) → $7,560 proceeds, hold 12 shares into Q2 earnings (Aug estimate). Defense supply chain contracts may drive $75-80 breakout.

WFC: At $80 (4.7% from current), sell 36 shares (90%) → $2,880 proceeds, retain 4 shares for asset cap removal announcement speculation under Warsh regime.

**CATALYST CALENDAR IMPACT**

**PCE May 28:** <2.8% bullish for financials (confirms soft landing), >3.0% triggers 3-5% sector pullback (buying opportunity). MP insulated from inflation data.

**FOMC Jun 16-17:** Warsh first meeting as Chair—expect hawkish structural rhetoric but no rate change. Bullish for bank NIMs if "higher for longer" confirmed.

**BAC Jul 14 / WFC Jul 15:** NIM expansion guidance key metric. Expect EPS beats 8-12% on interest income. Post-earnings profit-taking likely creates re-entry opportunity.

**STRATEGIC RECOMMENDATION**

**Bull 20%:** BAC $56, MP $72, WFC $82 | Portfolio $20,400 (+10%)
**Base 50%:** BAC $53, MP $66, WFC $78 | Portfolio $19,100 (+3%)
**Bear 25%:** BAC $49, MP $60, WFC $72 | Portfolio $17,000 (-8.4%)

Maintain positions. Execute Thorp tranche discipline at targets. Monitor PCE May 28 for macro inflection.

**Chief Strategist — BlueLotus Fund | Singapore

════ DOCUMENT 2: CHIEF STRATEGIST REPORT ════
Date: May 23, 2026 | Time: 16:39 SGT

**EXECUTIVE SUMMARY**
BlueLotus portfolio maintains +3.17% alpha with bullish positioning across financial services (BAC, WFC) and critical materials (MP) as neutral market regime creates bifurcated opportunity set. Strategic posture remains offense-capable with defensive optionality, positioned for July earnings catalysts while monitoring Iran/Fed tail risks through probabilistic scenario framework.

**STRATEGIC ASSESSMENT — 8-LENS VIEW**

**L1 Fundamental**: Bank earnings Jul 14-15 represent primary catalyst with NIM expansion potential from treasury yield surge creating 8-12% EPS upside surprise probability; MP fundamentals strengthening on China rare earth decoupling and defense sector demand acceleration, though valuation stretched at 2.8x sales vs. 2.2x historical average.

**L2 Technical**: Portfolio constituents displaying healthy uptrends with BAC establishing support at $51.00 (+2.9% from entry), MP consolidating gains at $64.43 (+4.0%), WFC grinding toward $77.00 resistance (+1.9%); quantum computing sector (QBTS +3.8%, RGTI +3.1%) showing parabolic characteristics warranting tactical watchlist positioning.

**L3 Macro**: Fed Chair Warsh regime transition creating uncertainty around June 16-17 FOMC policy stance; treasury yield surge (CNBC sentiment +0.743) compressing equity risk premium while VIX complacency at 16.7 suggests mispriced volatility; PCE May 28 critical inflection for 25bp July cut probability (currently 38%).

**L4 Geopolitical**: Iran ceasefire fragility dominates tail risk with Tehran claiming US violations while Trump signals negotiating patience; Hormuz closure probability elevated to 8% if peace talks fail by month-end, creating $120-140/bbl oil shock scenario with 200-300bp equity market drawdown potential.

**L5 Sentiment**: Fear & Greed precisely neutral at 50/100 while consumer sentiment hits record lows (-0.758) — divergence suggests either institutional complacency or smart money accumulation phase; retail capitulation not yet evident in flow data.

**L6 Sector Rotation**: Aggressive flows into quantum computing (+1.3% to +3.8%), space infrastructure (LUNR +2.2%, ASTS +1.0%), and semiconductors (ARM +1.1%); defensive rotation out of fuel cells (FCEL -2.5%) and legacy tech (MU -1.2%, AXON -1.3%) signals risk-on within growth sectors.

**L7 Institutional**: Nvidia $18.6B VC deployment signals conviction in AI/quantum infrastructure buildout; CNBC bond desk highlighting treasury yield competition for equity flows — institutional reallocation risk if 10Y exceeds 4.5%; no major downgrades across portfolio holdings.

**L8 News Flow**: High-velocity Iran narrative (-0.758 sentiment) creating headline risk while Fed plumbing reform speculation (0.000 neutral) circulates without immediate market impact; energy shock recession fears (2008 comparison) premature per RBC analysis given US energy independence improvements.

**ALADDIN PORTFOLIO OPTIMIZATION (A3)**
Current concentration: Financials 61.4% (BAC $7,552 + WFC $3,056), Materials 16.0% (MP $7,732) — within risk tolerance but elevated. Kelly criterion suggests 18-22% position sizing for 55% edge probability with 1.8:1 reward/risk; current BAC 40.7% exceeds optimal by ~20%, warranting profit-taking at $56 (90% rule) while riding 10% to $62 free. MP approaching optimal concentration ceiling; WFC undersized at 16.5%, opportunity to scale to $4,500 (24%) on pullback.
